intro: Qaisera carinata, also known as African boxwood, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Qaisera carinata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Qaisera carinata include decoctions and infusions, as well as ointments and creams, which are used to treat various health conditions and skin problems.
